posts.text:
New from me: Less than 5 percent of DA's offices have a unit tasked with seeking out wrongful convictions. I spoke with Marissa Bluestine of the Quattrone Center about what these units do well, what they could do better, and the obstacles they face.

(Link follows)

posts.text:
Despite (or because of) their success, CIUs now get pushback from Republican attorneys general, police departments who don't like to see their incompetence/corruption exposed, and city officials who fear the lawsuits wrought by exonerations.

Link:

radleybalko.substack.com/p/t…

posts.text:
The Trump Administration attempted to make an example of Minnesota during Operation Metro Surge.

Instead, Minnesotans set an example for the rest of the country that decency and compassion will always triumph over fear and division.

www.cbsnews.com/minnesota/ne...

posts.text: Absolute insanity - the Trump administration wants to pay nearly $1 billion of taxpayer dollars to a private French company to *not* build wind farms off the East Coast
posts.quote: Absolute insanity - the Trump administration wants to pay nearly $1 billion of taxpayer dollars to a private French company to *not* build wind farms off the East Coast

posts.text: ICE detention deaths are now occurring at a rate of roughly one every four days. Three weeks ago that rate was one every six days. The pace is accelerating, and Congress has not launched a single investigation.
posts.quote: ICE detention deaths are now occurring at a rate of roughly one every four days. Three weeks ago that rate was one every six days. The pace is accelerating, and Congress has not launched a single investigation.

posts.text: Awful. A teenager, dead by suicide in an ICE detention center. There is now a crisis of suicide inside the detention system; I have never seen anything like it. Suicide has happened before, but they were very rare. This is the third or fourth this year.
posts.quote: Awful. A teenager, dead by suicide in an ICE detention center. There is now a crisis of suicide inside the detention system; I have never seen anything like it. Suicide has happened before, but they were very rare. This is the third or fourth this year.

posts.text: We put the guy who wrote a book called "American Crusade" in charge of the most powerful military on earth. And now he's using his position to demand that Christians support his war against a Muslim country.
posts.quote: We put the guy who wrote a book called "American Crusade" in charge of the most powerful military on earth. And now he's using his position to demand that Christians support his war against a Muslim country.

posts.text:
In just one example of the entirely preventable crisis facing Dreamers, the admin abducted a #DACA recipient as he was on his way to visit his newborn in NICU.

Juan Chavez Velasco told agents that he has DACA. “That doesn’t matter,” they responded.

posts.text: Anyone else think it's highly inappropriate for the defense secretary to say "in the name of Jesus Christ" while he's doing a briefing about the U.S. killing people?

posts.text: Is $200 billion a lot to fight a war that’s already been decisively, comprehensively, bigly won? It seems like a lot.
posts.quote: Is $200 billion a lot to fight a war that’s already been decisively, comprehensively, bigly won? It seems like a lot.

posts.text:
This is a good time to remember that immigration judges aren’t real judges. 

They work for DOJ as part of the executive branch (under Bondi and Trump) and the fact that we call them judges like they’re independent arbiters would be laughable in a free and fair country.

posts.text: ICE agents detained a Texas father trying to deliver milk to his infant daughter in the neonatal intensive care unit, disregarding his status as a DACA recipient. trib.al/BOeMew3

Barack Obama (@BarackObama)

This interview, which is also available to listen to as a podcast, provides a useful overview of some of the dangerous trends we’ve been seeing in recent months regarding federalization and militarization of state and local police functions. nytimes.com/2025/08/27/o

New from me: Less than 5 percent of DA's offices have a unit tasked with seeking out wrongful convictions. I spoke with Marissa Bluestine of the Quattrone Center about what these units do well, what they could do better, and the obstacles they face.

(Link follows)

Despite (or because of) their success, CIUs now get pushback from Republican attorneys general, police departments who don't like to see their incompetence/corruption exposed, and city officials who fear the lawsuits wrought by exonerations.

immigration.quote:
The Trump Administration attempted to make an example of Minnesota during Operation Metro Surge.

Instead, Minnesotans set an example for the rest of the country that decency and compassion will always triumph over fear and division.

www.cbsnews.com/minnesota/ne...

ICE detention deaths are now occurring at a rate of roughly one every four days. Three weeks ago that rate was one every six days. The pace is accelerating, and Congress has not launched a single investigation.

Awful. A teenager, dead by suicide in an ICE detention center. There is now a crisis of suicide inside the detention system; I have never seen anything like it. Suicide has happened before, but they were very rare. This is the third or fourth this year.

In just one example of the entirely preventable crisis facing Dreamers, the admin abducted a #DACA recipient as he was on his way to visit his newborn in NICU.

Juan Chavez Velasco told agents that he has DACA. “That doesn’t matter,” they responded.

This is a good time to remember that immigration judges aren’t real judges. 

They work for DOJ as part of the executive branch (under Bondi and Trump) and the fact that we call them judges like they’re independent arbiters would be laughable in a free and fair country.

military-defense.quote: We put the guy who wrote a book called "American Crusade" in charge of the most powerful military on earth. And now he's using his position to demand that Christians support his war against a Muslim country.
Anyone else think it's highly inappropriate for the defense secretary to say "in the name of Jesus Christ" while he's doing a briefing about the U.S. killing people?
Is $200 billion a lot to fight a war that’s already been decisively, comprehensively, bigly won? It seems like a lot.
